title: StorageController forward-progress / liveness
description:
## Summary

Investigated and fixed a forward-progress / liveness bug in the
`StorageController`: visits could be stranded **perpetually in-progress** --
records cached but never committed and never marked incomplete -- so the visit
reached no terminal state and the work-queue client never saw completion. Seen
in production parquet->S3 crawls.

## Root cause (confirmed by reproduction)

A single **transient** structured-storage write error (e.g. an S3 PutObject
failure) was fatal to forward progress:

- `openwpm/storage/arrow_storage.py` `flush_cache`: wrote batches table-by-table
  and only afterwards cleared `_batches` / set the per-finalize `flush_events`.
  A `write_table` exception partway through stranded the cached records and left
  the finalize tokens unresolved -> visits perpetually in-progress.
- `openwpm/storage/storage_controller.py` `save_batch_if_past_timeout`: the only
  periodic flush driver during a live crawl, with no try/except around
  `flush_cache`. One transient error killed the coroutine permanently -> no more
  flushes -> finalized visits never flushed.
- `storage_controller.py` `shutdown`: unguarded final drain flush; a transient
  error aborted the drain and could deadlock on the never-resolving token.

Confirmed via direct in-process reproduction: a `write_table` that fails once
leaves `token.done() == False` forever and kills the timeout-flush task.

## Fix (PR #1197, branch `fix/storage-forward-progress`)

- `ArrowProvider.flush_cache` made atomic: write all tables, then (only on full
  success) clear batches and set events; on failure keep both and re-raise so a
  later flush retries cleanly.
- `save_batch_if_past_timeout` survives transient flush errors (log + retry next
  tick).
- Shutdown drain flush retried a bounded number of times (`SHUTDOWN_FLUSH_RETRIES`).

## Property tests (deliverable)

`test/storage/test_storage_controller_liveness.py` -- Hypothesis tests over
arbitrary interleavings (start / finalize success/failure / never-finalize /
concurrent visits / transient write failures), asserting invariants I1-I4
(committed-on-success, terminal-state-or-not-stranded, shutdown-drains,
bounded-cache). `test_forward_progress_survives_transient_write_failures`
reproduces the original bug (red pre-fix, green post-fix). All 4 green; existing
storage tests unaffected; `jj-precommit` (isort/black/mypy) green.

## Documented contract boundary (needs-human-awareness, not a defect)

A visit that emits **zero** records **and** is never finalized is invisible to
the `StorageController` -- it has nothing to key on. Driving such a
claimed-but-silent visit to a terminal state is the **work-queue client's**
responsibility (e.g. a visit-claim timeout). The tests encode this boundary.

## Status

Fixed and landed as draft PR #1197. Property tests are the executable spec of
the forward-progress guarantee.
